Based on the web search results, here is information about the Eiffel Tower's completion and inauguration:

## Completion and Inauguration Year: 1889

The construction of the Eiffel Tower structure was completed on Sunday, March 31, 1889, and was inaugurated at 1:30 p.m. while workers were still working on the interior fittings and the elevators hadn't been installed yet.

Construction of the Eiffel Tower began in 1887 and was finished in record time for a structure of its size – 2 years, 2 months and 5 days.

Gustave Eiffel raised a French flag at the top of the Tower in the presence of the Director of the World's Fair and some of his employees, and certain personalities from the Government and the City of Paris.

Following the initial inauguration ceremony, the Tower opened to the public on May 15, without even one elevator yet in operation. The elevators went into service on May 26!

The Eiffel Tower remained the world's tallest man-made structure until the completion of the Chrysler Building in New York in 1930.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

model_output:
The Eiffel Tower was completed and officially inaugurated in 1889, specifically on March 31, 1889 ([source](https://www.history.com/this-day-in-history/march-31/eiffel-tower-opens)).
